title: Deployment
---

# Deployment

Production-style setups run **one** service: **`clawql-mcp-http`** exposes MCP at **`/mcp`** and GraphQL at **`/graphql`** on the same port. 

## Streamable HTTP

From the published package:

```bash
PORT=8080 npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp-http
```

Default local MCP endpoint: **`http://localhost:8080/mcp`**, health **`/healthz`**. Your real URL depends on port, ingress, or tunneling‚Äîconfigure the client with **`url`**, not **`command`**.

## Optional gRPC (protobuf MCP)

The same registered tools (including **`CallTool`**) can be exposed over **gRPC** in the **same process** as Streamable HTTP. Set **`ENABLE_GRPC=1`**; the server listens on **`GRPC_PORT`** (default **50051**) using the **[`mcp-grpc-transport`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/mcp-grpc-transport)** package (`model_context_protocol.Mcp`, `grpc.health.v1.Health`, optional reflection). Clients must send **`mcp-protocol-version`** metadata on protobuf RPCs. Invoking from the shell: **[`packages/mcp-grpc-transport` README ‚Äî Testing and invoking MCP over gRPC](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/packages/mcp-grpc-transport/README.md#testing-and-invoking-mcp-over-grpc)**. On **Kubernetes**, the **`clawql-mcp-http`** Service publishes **HTTP** and **gRPC (50051)** so agents can reach **`localhost:50051`** (or the LoadBalancer IP) without port-forward when gRPC is enabled‚Äîsee [Kubernetes](/kubernetes) and [`docs/deploy-k8s.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/deploy-k8s.md#service-ports-http-and-grpc).

## Docker

The repo ships a multi-stage **Distroless** image (`docker/README.md`) with `dist/`, `bin/`, and `providers/` for offline bundled specs. Build, run, and compose examples:

[`github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md)

## Cloud Run

One-shot script (sets project/region via env):

```bash
PROJECT_ID="your-project-id" \
REGION="us-central1" \
bash scripts/deploy-cloud-run.sh
```

Or with Make:

```bash
PROJECT_ID="your-project-id" REGION="us-central1" make deploy-cloud-run
```

After deploy, agents use **`https://<mcp-service-url>/mcp`**. Full options: [`docs/deploy-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/deploy-cloud-run.md).

## Kubernetes

**Local MCP on Docker Desktop** (namespace `clawql`, **`http://localhost:8080/mcp`**, GHCR image or optional local build) is covered on **[Kubernetes](/kubernetes)**.

For **remote** clusters, use Kustomize overlays with image tag injection:

```bash
ENV=dev IMAGE=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/<project>/<repo>/clawql-mcp TAG=<tag> make deploy-k8s
```

Or install with **Helm** from **`charts/clawql-mcp`**: **[Helm](/helm)** and [`docs/helm.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/helm.md).

Details: [`docs/deploy-k8s.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/deploy-k8s.md). Extended container and manifest notes: [`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md).
/graphql-proxy:
---
title: GraphQL layer
---

# GraphQL layer

Single-spec **`execute`** runs **OpenAPI‚ÜíGraphQL** **in the same process** as **`clawql-mcp`** / **`clawql-mcp-http`**, so responses can be **field-projected** before they reach the model‚Äîsmaller payloads than many raw REST bodies. Streamable HTTP also exposes **`/graphql`** on the MCP port for debugging. 

## Same process as MCP

**`search`** and **`execute`** share one runtime. **`execute`** builds the GraphQL schema from the loaded spec, resolves the operation field, and proxies to upstream REST with **`@omnigraph/openapi`** (same engine as [GraphQL Mesh OpenAPI](https://the-guild.dev/graphql/mesh/docs/handlers/openapi)). Upstream calls use spec **`servers`** or **`CLAWQL_API_BASE_URL`**.

## Multi-spec and fallback

* **Merged specs:** **`execute`** uses **REST** per owning document; GraphQL is not the execution path for that mode today.
* **Single spec:** GraphQL is preferred when the schema builds; on translation or runtime errors, **`execute` falls back to REST** with the same operation map.

Pregenerated **`introspection.json`** (bundled or **`CLAWQL_INTROSPECTION_PATH`**) speeds field matching at MCP startup.

# Helm

The repository includes a **Helm 3** chart at **`charts/clawql-mcp`** that deploys **`clawql-mcp-http`**: Streamable HTTP MCP on **`/mcp`**, health on **`/healthz`**, GraphQL on **`/graphql`**, and (optionally) gRPC on port **50051**‚Äîmatching the [Kustomize](/kubernetes) layout.

## Install

From a **clone** of the repo (chart path is relative):

```bash
helm upgrade --install clawql ./charts/clawql-mcp \
  --namespace clawql \
  --create-namespace \
  --wait
```

The default image is **`ghcr.io/danielsmithdevelopment/clawql-mcp:latest`**. Override **`image.tag`** or **`image.repository`** for your registry.

**Docker Desktop:** **`make local-k8s-up`** runs Helm with **`values-docker-desktop.yaml`** (see [Kubernetes](/kubernetes)).

## Access bundled docs locally

With Docker Desktop defaults, the Helm install can deploy this docs UI (`website`) and expose it with ingress-nginx.

* **Docs UI:** `http://clawql.localhost`
* **MCP URL:** `http://localhost:8080/mcp`

Quick check:

```bash
curl -s http://clawql.localhost/api/health
```

Expected response includes `{"status":"ok"}`.

## Docs and values

* **Full guide** (examples: ClusterIP, gRPC, tokens, Ingress, persistence): **[`docs/helm.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/helm.md)** on GitHub
* **Default values:** [`charts/clawql-mcp/values.yaml`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/charts/clawql-mcp/values.yaml)
* **Docker Desktop preset:** [`charts/clawql-mcp/values-docker-desktop.yaml`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/charts/clawql-mcp/values-docker-desktop.yaml)

## Helm vs Kustomize

|             | [Kubernetes / Kustomize](/kubernetes)       | Helm (this page)                                                           |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Install** | `kubectl apply -k`, `make deploy-k8s`       | `helm upgrade --install`, **`make local-k8s-up`** (Docker Desktop default) |
| **Config**  | Overlays under `docker/kustomize/overlays/` | `values.yaml`, **`values-docker-desktop.yaml`**, **`--set`**               |

**Docker Desktop:** **`make local-k8s-up`** defaults to Helm. For **`kubectl apply -k`** instead, use **`CLAWQL_LOCAL_K8S_INSTALLER=kustomize`** (see [Kubernetes](/kubernetes)). Remote **`dev` / `prod`** often use **`scripts/deploy-k8s.sh`**.

## Validate the chart locally

```bash
make helm-lint
```

# Install

ClawQL is published as **`clawql-mcp`** on npm. The package ships bundled OpenAPI and Discovery specs under `providers/` so discovery works offline‚Äîexpect a **large install** (\~90+ MB on disk) compared to a typical small CLI. 

## npm / yarn / bun

```bash
npm install clawql-mcp
# yarn add clawql-mcp
# bun add clawql-mcp
```

Registry tarballs are \~10 MB compressed; installed size is dominated by bundled specs.

## Binaries

After install, these commands are on your `PATH` (or use `npx -p clawql-mcp <binary>`):

| Command           | Purpose                                                               |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `clawql-mcp`      | MCP server on **stdio** (Cursor, Claude Desktop, etc.)                |
| `clawql-mcp-http` | MCP over **Streamable HTTP** (`PORT`, `/mcp`, `/healthz`, `/graphql`) |

## Run without a global install

```bash
CLAWQL_PROVIDER=all-providers npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

Point your MCP client at **`clawql-mcp`** on stdio (see [MCP clients](/mcp-clients)).

## Docker and HTTP

* **Docker:** multi-stage image and stdio/compose notes live in the repo‚Äôs [`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md).
* **Remote MCP:** `PORT=8080 npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp-http` ‚Äî default local endpoint `http://localhost:8080/mcp` (health: `/healthz`, GraphQL: `/graphql`). See [Deployment](/deployment).

## Install from GitHub

If you install from git instead of the registry, run **`npm run build`** once so `dist/` exists (the published npm tarball already includes `dist/`).

```bash
npm i github:danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL
npm run build
```

---
title: Kubernetes
---

# Kubernetes

ClawQL is usually deployed as **`clawql-mcp-http`**: **Streamable HTTP** MCP on **`/mcp`**, **GraphQL** on **`/graphql`**, and (when **`ENABLE_GRPC=1`**) **gRPC** protobuf MCP on port **50051**‚Äîall in one pod. The **Service** exposes **both** HTTP and **gRPC** ports so clients can use **`localhost:50051`** (Docker Desktop) or **`<LoadBalancer-ip>:50051`** without **`kubectl port-forward`**. **Docker Desktop** defaults to the **[Helm](/helm)** chart (**`charts/clawql-mcp`**, **`values-docker-desktop.yaml`**); remote clusters often use **Kustomize** **`dev`** / **`prod`** with **`scripts/deploy-k8s.sh`**. 

## Local MCP on Docker Desktop

Use this when you want a **stable URL** (for example **`http://localhost:8080/mcp`**) for Cursor or other MCP clients, without running `npx` in a terminal.

### Prerequisites

* Docker Desktop with **Kubernetes** enabled (Settings ‚Üí Kubernetes).
* **`kubectl`** on your PATH. **Helm 3** is required for the **default** install path only.
* Repo clone at the version you want to run.

### One-shot install

From the **repository root**:

```bash
make local-k8s-up
```

Or:

```bash
bash scripts/local-k8s-docker-desktop.sh
```

**Default:** **`helm upgrade --install`** with **`charts/clawql-mcp/values-docker-desktop.yaml`**: **`ghcr.io/danielsmithdevelopment/clawql-mcp:latest`**, LoadBalancer on **8080**, **`all-providers`**, **`hostPath`** vault at **`$HOME/.ClawQL`** (override **`CLAWQL_LOCAL_VAULT_HOST_PATH`**).

**Kustomize:** **`CLAWQL_LOCAL_K8S_INSTALLER=kustomize make local-k8s-up`** ‚Äî **`kubectl apply -k docker/kustomize/overlays/local`** (no Helm). To **build locally** instead of GHCR, run **`CLAWQL_LOCAL_K8S_BUILD_IMAGE=1 make local-k8s-up`** (works with either installer).

### Endpoints and client config

* **MCP (Streamable HTTP):** `http://localhost:8080/mcp`
* **Health:** `curl -s http://localhost:8080/healthz`
* **Bundled docs UI (Helm + UI ingress):** `http://clawql.localhost`

Point your MCP client at **`url`** (not stdio **`command`**). Example shape:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "clawql": {
      "url": "http://localhost:8080/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

Use your real host if you tunnel or expose the service elsewhere.

### Optional gRPC on the same deployment

When **`ENABLE_GRPC=1`** (and optionally **`ENABLE_GRPC_REFLECTION=1`** for **`grpcurl list`**), the container listens on **50051** for **`grpc.health.v1`**, **`model_context_protocol.Mcp`**, and **`mcp.transport.v1.Mcp.Session`**. The **`clawql-mcp-http`** Service includes a **`grpc`** port (**50051**) alongside HTTP in the Helm chart and Kustomize **base** / **dev** / **prod**‚Äîverify with **`kubectl -n clawql get svc clawql-mcp-http`**. For native **`grpc`** readiness/liveness probes on kubelet 1.27+, use the opt-in overlay **`docker/kustomize/overlays/grpc-enabled/`** (see root [**README**](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/README.md) Kubernetes section). Full port matrix: [`docs/deploy-k8s.md` ‚Äî Service ports](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/deploy-k8s.md#service-ports-http-and-grpc).

### What the Helm install sets

* **Namespace:** `clawql`
* **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER`:** **`all-providers`** (Google Cloud bundled APIs + every other bundled vendor; same as bare `npx clawql-mcp` with no spec env). Override via **`values-docker-desktop.yaml`** or **`helm --set provider=...`**.
* **Kube context:** the script prefers **`docker-desktop`** when present so you do not accidentally deploy to another cluster.

Cold start can take a while while specs load. Wait until **`/healthz`** returns **`{"status":"ok",...}`** or the pod is **Ready** before the client connects.

### MCP auth tokens (merged default bundle)

For **local Docker Desktop k8s**, `execute` needs tokens in the **`clawql-mcp-http`** pod. Per-vendor env vars match **`src/auth-headers.ts`**: GitHub (**`CLAWQL_GITHUB_TOKEN`**), Cloudflare (**`CLAWQL_CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`**), Google (**`GOOGLE_ACCESS_TOKEN`** / **`CLAWQL_GOOGLE_ACCESS_TOKEN`**), Slack and other fallbacks (**`CLAWQL_BEARER_TOKEN`** / **`CLAWQL_HTTP_HEADERS`**), Paperless (**`PAPERLESS_API_TOKEN`**), Stirling (**`STIRLING_API_KEY`**), optional bearer for Tika/Gotenberg. The same GitHub PAT is often stored as **`CLAWQL_BEARER_TOKEN`** for merged-bundle fallback.

**One-shot:** from the repo root, with **`gh`** logged in **or** values in **`.env`**:

```bash
bash scripts/k8s-docker-desktop-set-mcp-auth.sh
```

Pipe a PAT: `gh auth token | bash scripts/k8s-docker-desktop-set-mcp-auth.sh`. Set **`CLAWQL_LOAD_DOTENV=0`** to skip sourcing **`.env`**.

This creates/updates Secret **`clawql-github-auth`** (name kept for backward compatibility), attaches keys to **`deployment/clawql-mcp-http`**, and restarts. Full table and manual **`kubectl`** steps: **[`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md)** (section *MCP auth*). The old script name **`k8s-docker-desktop-set-github-token.sh`** is a deprecated alias.

**`helm upgrade --install`** reapplies chart values; env injected only with **`kubectl set env`** can be overwritten on the next upgrade. Prefer **`helm --set extraEnv`** or a **Secret** wired through **`values.yaml`** for durable config.

### Rebuild after code changes

After you change the repo, either rebuild locally (**`CLAWQL_LOCAL_K8S_BUILD_IMAGE=1 make local-k8s-up`**) or pull a new GHCR tag and run **`helm upgrade`** with **`image.tag`**. Example recycle:

```bash
make local-k8s-up
kubectl --context docker-desktop -n clawql rollout restart deployment/clawql-mcp-http
```

### Teardown

```bash
helm uninstall clawql -n clawql
```

Or delete the whole namespace:

```bash
kubectl --context docker-desktop delete namespace clawql
```

## Remote clusters (dev / prod)

For registries and image tags (not the local **`latest`** flow), use overlays and the deploy script:

```bash
ENV=dev IMAGE=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/<project>/<repo>/clawql-mcp TAG=<tag> make deploy-k8s
```

Or install the **[Helm](/helm)** chart with your image registry. Full checklist: [`docs/deploy-k8s.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/deploy-k8s.md).

## More detail in the repo

* Container build, Compose, and extended K8s notes: [`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docker/README.md)
* Starter manifest: `docker/kubernetes-starter.yaml`

title: Slack `notify`
---

# Slack `notify`

The optional **`notify`** MCP tool sends messages to Slack with **`chat.postMessage`**. Use it for **workflow milestones**, **batch completions**, and **alerts** so agents do not need to call **`execute("chat_postMessage", ‚Ä¶)`** for every post.

* **Enable:** **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_NOTIFY=1`** (or `true` / `yes`).
* **Spec:** The loaded OpenAPI merge must include **Slack** (operation id **`chat_postMessage`**), e.g. **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER=slack`**, **`CLAWQL_BUNDLED_PROVIDERS`** containing **`slack`**, or a default merge that already includes Slack.
* **Auth:** **`CLAWQL_SLACK_TOKEN`** / **`SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`** / ‚Ä¶ ‚Äî same rules as **`execute`** for the **`slack`** label. Minimum scope **`chat:write`**.

**Canonical guide (setup, `notify` vs `execute`, thread replies, Block Kit, errors, Cursor JSON, Helm):** [notify-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/notify-tool.md) ¬∑ [mcp-tools.md ¬ß notify](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#notify-optional) ¬∑ issue [#77](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/77).

## Quick examples

Minimal completion message:

```json
{
  "channel": "C0123456789",
  "text": "‚úÖ Q1 invoice import finished ‚Äî 14 files archived."
}
```

Thread reply (use the parent message **`ts`** from Slack):

```json
{
  "channel": "C0123456789",
  "thread_ts": "1713898000.000100",
  "text": "Step 3/3: import complete ‚Äî doc id `5102`."
}
```

Public channel by name (bot must be a member):

```json
{
  "channel": "#releases",
  "text": "*Deploy* succeeded for `v4.0.0`."
}
```

Slack sometimes returns **`{"ok":false,"error":"not_in_channel"}`** with HTTP 200. The **`notify`** tool maps that to a tool result **`error`** string plus the parsed **`slack`** object so the model can retry or ask you to **`/invite`** the bot.

## Testing

Repo tests: **`src/clawql-notify.test.ts`**, **`src/server.test.ts`**. Follow-up issues: [#136](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/136)‚Äì[#140](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/140) ‚Äî index **[notify-tool-test-backlog.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/notify-tool-test-backlog.md)**.

See also [Tools](/tools) for how **`notify`** fits alongside **`search`**, **`execute`**, **`memory_*`**, **`cache`**, and **`audit`**.
/onyx-knowledge:
---
title: Onyx `knowledge_search_onyx`
---

# Onyx `knowledge_search_onyx`

The optional **`knowledge_search_onyx`** MCP tool queries your **[Onyx](https://www.onyx.app/)** index via **`POST /search/send-search-message`**. It is a thin wrapper over the same **`execute`** path as operation **`onyx::onyx_send_search_message`** when the **`onyx`** bundled spec is in the merge ‚Äî so you get **typed parameters** (`query`, `num_hits`, ‚Ä¶) without hand-building the Onyx JSON each time.

* **Enable:** **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_ONYX=1`** (or `true` / `yes`).
* **Spec:** The loaded merge must include **`onyx`** (default **`all-providers`**, or add **`onyx`** to **`CLAWQL_BUNDLED_PROVIDERS`**, or **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER=onyx`** for Onyx-only).
* **Connectivity:** **`ONYX_BASE_URL`** = HTTP API root (often ends with **`/api`**). **Auth:** **`ONYX_API_TOKEN`** or **`CLAWQL_ONYX_API_TOKEN`** as **Bearer**, or **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER_AUTH_JSON`** with an **`onyx`** key.

**Canonical guide (setup, wrapper vs `execute`, examples, errors, `memory_ingest` / `notify`):** [onyx-knowledge-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/onyx-knowledge-tool.md) ¬∑ [mcp-tools.md ¬ß knowledge\_search\_onyx](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#knowledge_search_onyx-optional) ¬∑ issue [#118](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/118).

## Quick examples

Minimal retrieval:

```json
{
  "query": "What is our data retention policy for EU customers?"
}
```

Limit hits and trim top-level JSON keys (adjust **`fields`** to match your Onyx response):

```json
{
  "query": "runbook kubernetes cluster upgrade",
  "num_hits": 8,
  "include_content": true,
  "fields": ["query", "documents"]
}
```

Equivalent **`execute`** (merged **`onyx`** label):

```json
{
  "operationId": "onyx::onyx_send_search_message",
  "args": {
    "search_query": "deployment runbook kubernetes",
    "num_hits": 10,
    "include_content": true,
    "stream": false
  }
}
```

## Vault and Slack

* After retrieval, use **`memory_ingest`** to persist a short summary and redacted hit list so **`memory_recall`** can surface it in later sessions ‚Äî see [vault memory](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/memory-obsidian.md).
* Use optional **`notify`** to post completion signals with links ‚Äî [Slack notify](/notify).

## Tests

Repo tests: **`src/knowledge-search-onyx.test.ts`**, **`src/server.test.ts`** (tool registered when **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_ONYX=1`**).

See [Tools](/tools) for how **`knowledge_search_onyx`** fits alongside **`search`**, **`execute`**, vault tools, **`cache`**, **`audit`**, and **`notify`**.

# Ouroboros library (`clawql-ouroboros`)

ClawQL ships a TypeScript workspace package **`clawql-ouroboros`** for **specification-first** multi-generation workflows: validated **Seeds**, **Wonder / Reflect** hooks, pluggable **Executor** and **Evaluator**, **ontology convergence** (similarity, stagnation, oscillation, regression gates), an **`EvolutionaryLoop`**, and helpers for **MCP-style tool registration** and a **background seed poller**. 

It is a **library** you `import` from Node ‚â• 22 ‚Äî it is **not** registered on the **`clawql-mcp`** MCP server yet. Slides and roadmap may describe ‚Äúinvisible Ouroboros‚Äù inside ClawQL; this package is the **current code artifact** toward that story.

## How it relates to upstream ‚ÄúOuroboros‚Äù

The widely known Python project **[Q00/ouroboros](https://github.com/Q00/ouroboros)** is a full agentic stack (interview, CLI, MCP plugin, execution routing, persistence, and more). **`clawql-ouroboros`** is a **ClawQL-scoped subset**: overlapping concepts (seed, wonder/reflect, convergence), **not** API or feature parity. The npm name **`clawql-ouroboros`** avoids implying an official TypeScript port of Q00.

## Install and build

From the **ClawQL** monorepo, depend on the workspace:

```json
{ "dependencies": { "clawql-ouroboros": "workspace:*" } }
```

From **npm** (after you publish `clawql-ouroboros`):

```bash
npm install clawql-ouroboros
```

From a repo checkout, build the package:

```bash
npm run build -w clawql-ouroboros
```

## Imports

| Path                         | Contents                                                                                           |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `clawql-ouroboros`           | `SeedSchema`, `EvolutionaryLoop`, `ConvergenceCriteria`, `InMemoryEventStore`, types               |
| `clawql-ouroboros/mcp-hooks` | `ouroborosMcpTools` ‚Äî schemas + handlers for crystallize / run / lineage (wire to your MCP server) |
| `clawql-ouroboros/poller`    | `startSeedsPoller` ‚Äî interval loop with your DB `fetchPending` / `markFailed`                      |

## Minimal usage (conceptual)

1. Implement or inject **WonderEngine**, **ReflectEngine**, **Executor**, **Evaluator** (or use stubs for tests).
2. Choose an **EventStore** ‚Äî **`InMemoryEventStore`** for dev/tests; **Postgres** (or similar) for production (**you implement** `append` / `getLineage`).
3. Construct **`EvolutionaryLoop`**, then **`await loop.run(seed)`** or **`run(seed, { maxGenerations, convergenceThreshold })`** for per-run caps.

## npm registry

On **npm**, the package listing shows **`README.md`** from [`packages/clawql-ouroboros/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/packages/clawql-ouroboros/README.md) ‚Äî install, quick start, export table, and honest scope for **any** Node ‚â•22 project (not only ClawQL). The tarball includes **`LICENSE`** (Apache-2.0).

## Full guide and copy-paste examples

Canonical documentation with **examples** (minimal loop, convergence-only, MCP hooks, poller) lives in the repo:

**[clawql-ouroboros.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/clawql-ouroboros.md)**

Package README (short pointer): **[packages/clawql-ouroboros/README.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/packages/clawql-ouroboros/README.md)**.

## See also

* [Tools](/tools) ‚Äî MCP tools on **`clawql-mcp`** (**`search`**, **`execute`**, vault, optional **`notify`**, etc.).
* [Concepts](/concepts) ‚Äî how ClawQL‚Äôs **token-efficient** `search` ‚Üí `execute` path fits the broader agent story.
* [mcp-tools.md ‚Äî Workspace library section](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#workspace-library-clawql-ouroboros-not-an-mcp-tool-on-clawql-mcp-yet) ‚Äî one-page pointer next to env flags.

# Quickstart

You can be up and running in a few minutes with **`npx`**‚Äîno global install required. Run **`clawql-mcp`**; **`execute`** uses in-process OpenAPI‚ÜíGraphQL for single-spec APIs. 

## 1. Start the MCP server

```bash
npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

For every bundled vendor at once:

```bash
CLAWQL_PROVIDER=all-providers npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

## 2. Connect your client

Add an MCP server entry that runs **`clawql-mcp`** on stdio (see [MCP clients](/mcp-clients)). No separate GraphQL process or **`GRAPHQL_URL`** is required.

## 3. Use your own API spec

**Local file**

```bash
CLAWQL_SPEC_PATH=./openapi.yaml npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

**Remote OpenAPI**

```bash
CLAWQL_SPEC_URL=https://example.com/openapi.json npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

**Google Discovery**

```bash
CLAWQL_DISCOVERY_URL="https://compute.googleapis.com/\$discovery/rest?version=v1" npx -p clawql-mcp clawql-mcp
```

## 4. HTTP transport (optional)

For **`clawql-mcp-http`**, MCP is at **`/mcp`** and GraphQL at **`/graphql`** on the same **`PORT`** (see [Install](/install)).

## 5. Local bundled docs via Helm (Docker Desktop)

If you deploy locally with Kubernetes + Helm:

```bash
make local-k8s-up
```

You can open the bundled docs UI at **`http://clawql.localhost`** and keep MCP clients pointed to **`http://localhost:8080/mcp`**.

## Next steps

* [Install](/install) ‚Äî binaries, Docker, HTTP transport
* [Spec configuration](/spec-configuration) ‚Äî merge rules and environment variables
* [Tools](/tools) ‚Äî **`search`** and **`execute`**

title: Spec configuration
---

# Spec configuration

ClawQL builds an in-memory operation index from **OpenAPI 3** (JSON/YAML), **Swagger 2** (auto-upgraded), **Google Discovery** documents, or **bundled presets** under `providers/`. Selection follows a **two-stage** precedence implemented in `src/spec-loader.ts`. 

## Overview

* **Multi-spec mode:** one merged **search** index; **`execute`** uses **REST** per owning spec (see [GraphQL proxy](/graphql-proxy)).
* **Single-spec mode:** one document; **`execute`** can use the internal GraphQL layer when translation succeeds, with **REST fallback** on failure.

## Stage 1 ‚Äî Multi-spec merge

**User-facing model:** for multi-spec there is no hidden ‚Äúpartial‚Äù default. Either you rely on the **one** built-in unconfigured merge (**`all-providers`**, step 4), or you **choose** a merge (local paths, bundled id list, or a merged `CLAWQL_PROVIDER` shortcut). `CLAWQL_GOOGLE_TOP50_SPECS` / `CLAWQL_GOOGLE_CLOUD_SPECS` do not select a merge on their own.

Precedence (order matters) when a merge is selected or defaulted:

1. **`CLAWQL_SPEC_PATHS`** ‚Äî merge local files (comma, semicolon, or newline separated).
2. **`CLAWQL_BUNDLED_PROVIDERS`** ‚Äî comma/semicolon/newline-separated **bundled** provider ids and/or **`google`** (full GCP on-disk set).
3. **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER`** ‚Äî merged shortcut (`google`, `all-providers`, `atlassian`, ‚Ä¶) when not building a list yourself.
4. **Built-in default** ‚Äî only when no merge was selected: **`all-providers`**.

## Stage 2 ‚Äî Single spec

If Stage 1 does **not** apply, the first match wins:

1. **`CLAWQL_SPEC_PATH`** (aliases: `OPENAPI_SPEC_PATH`, `OPENAPI_FILE`)
2. **`CLAWQL_SPEC_URL`** (alias: `OPENAPI_SPEC_URL`)
3. **`CLAWQL_DISCOVERY_URL`** (alias: `GOOGLE_DISCOVERY_URL`)
4. **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER`** ‚Äî **single** bundled vendor (`cloudflare`, `github`, ‚Ä¶), not a merged preset
5. Rare fallback (`kind: "default"` in code)

**Examples:** `CLAWQL_SPEC_PATH=./openapi.yaml` skips Stage 1. `CLAWQL_PROVIDER=cloudflare` alone loads Cloudflare as a **single** bundle. An empty environment (nothing above) uses **`all-providers`**; for a custom subset, set **`CLAWQL_BUNDLED_PROVIDERS=‚Ä¶`**, not another implicit default.

## Environment variables

| Variable                    | Meaning                                                                                 |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `CLAWQL_SPEC_PATH`          | Local OpenAPI JSON/YAML                                                                 |
| `CLAWQL_SPEC_PATHS`         | Multiple paths ‚Üí **merge**                                                              |
| `CLAWQL_BUNDLED_PROVIDERS`  | Ids to merge: `BUNDLED_PROVIDERS` keys and/or `google`                                  |
| `CLAWQL_SPEC_URL`           | Fetch OpenAPI from HTTPS                                                                |
| `CLAWQL_DISCOVERY_URL`      | Google Discovery JSON URL                                                               |
| `CLAWQL_PROVIDER`           | Merged preset (Stage 1) or single vendor (Stage 2)                                      |
| `CLAWQL_PROVIDER_AUTH_JSON` | One JSON object: **`specLabel`** ‚Üí token string or headers (multi-vendor **`execute`**) |
| `CLAWQL_HTTP_HEADERS`       | Global header JSON; with **`CLAWQL_PROVIDER_AUTH_JSON`**, omit **`Authorization`** here |
| `CLAWQL_INTROSPECTION_PATH` | Pregenerated GraphQL introspection JSON (optional)                                      |
| `CLAWQL_API_BASE_URL`       | Override REST base URL                                                                  |
| `CLAWQL_BUNDLED_OFFLINE`    | `1` / `true` ‚Äî do not fetch missing bundled files from the network                      |

Full list: [`.env.example`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/.env.example) in the repo.

## Google Discovery

Use **`CLAWQL_DISCOVERY_URL`** for GCP and other Google APIs, for example:

```bash
export CLAWQL_DISCOVERY_URL="https://compute.googleapis.com/\$discovery/rest?version=v1"
```

The repo vendors a lookup snapshot and docs for refreshing it: [`docs/google-apis-lookup.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/google-apis-lookup.md). The **bundled Google Cloud** API set (on-disk manifest under [`providers/google/`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/tree/main/providers/google)) powers merged preset **`google`**.

title: Tools
---

# Tools

ClawQL always exposes **`search`** and **`execute`**; additional tools appear when optional features are enabled (see table below). The **data plane** for REST/Discovery APIs is **`search`** then **`execute`**. Agents call **`search`** to discover operations and parameters, then **`execute`** to run a chosen **`operationId`** with optional field selection. [Canonical reference with examples](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md). 

A **seventh** tool, **`cache`**, is available when **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_CACHE`** is set ‚Äî ephemeral in-process LRU storage, not the vault. See [Session cache](/cache) and [cache-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/cache-tool.md). An **eighth** optional tool, **`audit`**, is available when **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_AUDIT`** is set ‚Äî in-process ring buffer for structured events (not durable) ‚Äî [enterprise-mcp-tools.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/enterprise-mcp-tools.md). A **ninth** optional tool, **`notify`**, is available when **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_NOTIFY`** is set ‚Äî Slack **`chat.postMessage`** when the Slack spec is loaded ([#77](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/77)) ‚Äî [Slack notify](/notify) (quick examples), [notify-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/notify-tool.md) (full guide), [mcp-tools.md ¬ß notify](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#notify-optional). A **tenth** optional tool, **`knowledge_search_onyx`**, is available when **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_ONYX`** is set ‚Äî Onyx semantic document search when the **`onyx`** bundled provider is in the merge ([#118](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/118)) ‚Äî [Onyx knowledge search](/onyx-knowledge) (quick examples), [onyx-knowledge-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/onyx-knowledge-tool.md) (full guide), [mcp-tools.md ¬ß knowledge\_search\_onyx](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#knowledge_search_onyx-optional).

## `search`

Searches the active OpenAPI/Discovery corpus by **natural language intent** and returns a **short ranked list** of matching operations (paths, methods, parameters, summaries). Work happens against an **in-memory index** inside the MCP process‚Äîfull specs are not pasted into the model context.

## `execute`

Runs a discovered operation by **`operationId`** with arguments shaped from the spec. Internally, single-spec mode prefers the **GraphQL proxy** so only requested fields flow back; **multi-spec** and some translation failures use **REST** directly. Agents do **not** author GraphQL; the server builds the internal query.

## Typical workflow

```
1. search("delete a Cloud Run service")
   ‚Üí top hits with operationId, method, path, parameters

2. execute("run.projects.locations.services.delete", {
     name: "projects/my-proj/locations/us-central1/services/my-svc"
   })
   ‚Üí lean JSON (GraphQL-shaped or REST fallback)
```

See [GraphQL layer](/graphql-proxy) for how **`execute`** uses OpenAPI‚ÜíGraphQL in-process vs REST in multi-spec mode.

## Optional tools

| Tool                                  | Needs                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `sandbox_exec`                        | Deployed [sandbox bridge](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/tree/main/cloudflare/sandbox-bridge) Worker + `CLAWQL_SANDBOX_BRIDGE_URL` (remote sandbox; args include **`code`**)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `memory_ingest`, `memory_recall`      | `CLAWQL_OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH` (writable vault directory). **`memory_ingest`** can take optional **`toolOutputsFile`** (server-side UTF-8 read, path allowlist via **`CLAWQL_MEMORY_INGEST_FILE_*`**) for large verbatim bodies without huge MCP JSON ‚Äî [memory\_ingest](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#memory_ingest). Optional vector / hybrid DB: [memory-db-hybrid-implementation](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/memory-db-hybrid-implementation.md)                                                                                                                         |
| `ingest_external_knowledge`           | Set **`CLAWQL_EXTERNAL_INGEST=1`**: bulk Markdown (`documents[]`) and optional URL fetch (`CLAWQL_EXTERNAL_INGEST_FETCH=1`) ‚Äî [external-ingest.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/external-ingest.md)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `cache` (optional)                    | **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_CACHE=1`** ‚Äî in-process LRU key/value only; not persisted ‚Äî [cache-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/cache-tool.md), [Session cache](/cache)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `audit` (optional)                    | **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_AUDIT=1`** ‚Äî in-process event ring buffer; not on disk ‚Äî [enterprise-mcp-tools.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/enterprise-mcp-tools.md) ([#89](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/89))                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `notify` (optional)                   | **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_NOTIFY=1`** ‚Äî Slack **`chat.postMessage`**; Slack spec in merge + bot token (**`CLAWQL_SLACK_TOKEN`**, ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî [Slack notify](/notify), [notify-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/notify-tool.md), [mcp-tools.md ¬ß notify](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#notify-optional) ([#77](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/77))                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `knowledge_search_onyx` (optional)    | **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_ONYX=1`** ‚Äî Onyx **`POST /search/send-search-message`**; **`onyx`** in merge + **`ONYX_BASE_URL`** + **`ONYX_API_TOKEN`** (Bearer) ‚Äî [Onyx knowledge search](/onyx-knowledge), [onyx-knowledge-tool.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/onyx-knowledge-tool.md), [mcp-tools.md ¬ß knowledge\_search\_onyx](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md#knowledge_search_onyx-optional) ([#118](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/118))                                                                                                                  |
| **`ouroboros_*`** (optional, 3 tools) | **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_OUROBOROS=1`** ‚Äî **`ouroboros_create_seed_from_document`**, **`ouroboros_run_evolutionary_loop`**, **`ouroboros_get_lineage_status`** from **`clawql-ouroboros`**; optional **`CLAWQL_OUROBOROS_DATABASE_URL`** for Postgres-backed events ‚Äî [Ouroboros library](/ouroboros), [clawql-ouroboros.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/clawql-ouroboros.md), [mcp-tools.md ¬ß Ouroboros](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/mcp-tools.md) ([#141](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/141), [#142](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/issues/142)) |

They do not replace **`execute`** for calling upstream APIs (except **`notify`** and **`knowledge_search_onyx`**, which wrap single operations for convenience).

## Ouroboros (`clawql-ouroboros`)

The **`clawql-ouroboros`** package is an **evolutionary loop** library (Seed, Wonder/Reflect, Executor/Evaluator, convergence). With **`CLAWQL_ENABLE_OUROBOROS=1`**, **`clawql-mcp`** registers three **`ouroboros_*`** MCP tools backed by the same library; without the flag, import **`clawql-ouroboros`** from your own service. Overview, install, and **examples**: **[Ouroboros library](/ouroboros)** ¬∑ canonical **[clawql-ouroboros.md](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/clawql-ouroboros.md)**.

---
title: Troubleshooting
---

# Troubleshooting

ClawQL is designed around **spec-driven discovery** and a **lean execution path**. Most confusion comes from spec selection or expectations about retrieval quality on huge APIs. 

## GraphQL vs REST

In-process GraphQL uses **`@omnigraph/openapi`** (same family as [GraphQL Mesh OpenAPI](https://the-guild.dev/graphql/mesh/docs/handlers/openapi)). Some vendor specs hit **translation edge cases**.

**Behavior:** When GraphQL resolution fails, **`execute` falls back to REST** using the same OpenAPI operation map. **`search`** always uses the full OpenAPI-derived index.

**Multi-spec mode:** **`execute`** uses **REST** for merged corpora; GraphQL applies to single-spec paths where the schema builds cleanly.

## Search behavior

Search is intentionally **lightweight keyword scoring** over operation metadata (MVP). On **very large** specs you may see noisy rankings or misses; future work may swap in stronger retrieval.

## Large specs

ClawQL **normalizes** specs (refs, nullable patterns, shorthand `items`, empty servers) so large documents load reliably. The design goal is **keep the full spec**‚Äîefficiency comes from **`search` + selective `execute`**, not from deleting operations.

If startup or pregenerate steps OOM or fail, see maintainer notes in [`providers/README.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/providers/README.md) and optional **`CLAWQL_INTROSPECTION_PATH`**.

## Contributing upstream

To improve OpenAPI‚ÜíGraphQL translation for everyone, see [`docs/OPENAPI_TO_GRAPHQL_UPSTREAM.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/docs/OPENAPI_TO_GRAPHQL_UPSTREAM.md) and [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/danielsmithdevelopment/ClawQL/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
